tluaTableAsArray.tlua(27,7): error TS2741: Property 'length' is missing in type 'number[]' but required in type 'Array<number>'.


==== tluaTableAsArray.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // `T[]` denotes the number-key table type `{ [k: number]: T }`, so a table
    // constructor satisfies it and is indexed element-wise.
    local xs: number[] = {1, 2, 3};
    local first: number = xs[1];
    
    // A vararg has no name, so the pack is reached the Lua way: collect it into a
    // table with `{...}`, which is itself a number-key table and indexes
    // element-wise.
    function firstOf(...: string): string
      local args: string[] = {...};
      return args[1];
    end
    local f1: string = firstOf("a", "b", "c");
    
    // Generic vararg inference: T is inferred from the value arguments.
    function pickFirst<T>(...: T): T
      local items: T[] = {...};
      return items[1];
    end
    local n: number = pickFirst(1, 2, 3);
    
    // readonly T[] is the readonly number-key table.
    local ys: readonly number[] = {10, 20};
    
    // The global Array survives only as an inert, separately-nameable type: it is
    // distinct from T[], so a table is not assignable to it (missing length/methods).
    local arr: Array<number> = xs; // error
          ~~~
!!! error TS2741: Property 'length' is missing in type 'number[]' but required in type 'Array<number>'.
!!! related TS2728 lib.luajit.d.tlua:16:14: 'length' is declared here.
    
    // Recursive T[] resolves without diverging.
    type J = J[];
    local j: J = {};
    
    local es = {first, f1, n, ys, j};
